steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content: By the zero-product property, $6x(x-4)(x+8)=0$ requires one factor to be zero. That gives $x=0$, $x=4$, or $x=-8$.

steps.title: Identify a valid solution
steps.content: Among the choices, $0$ is a root of the equation. It works because substituting $x=0$ makes the factor $6x$ equal zero. B is correct.
